agreement_title: DQ Grill & Chill Operating Agreement (4025 G&C Oper Agmt – 3/25)
agreement_structure: 16 sections + definitions, ~40 pages + Undertaking and Guarantee, Ownership Addendum, Relocation Addendum, Renewal Addendum, State Addenda

grant_and_term.grant: Limited license to operate single DQ Grill & Chill restaurant at authorized location using Trademarks
grant_and_term.reservation_of_rights: Section 2.4 — ADQ reserves all rights not expressly granted, including right to operate/franchise competing businesses anywhere except authorized location
grant_and_term.initial_term: 20 years (new); lesser of 20 years or remaining transferor term (transfer); renewal term from expiring agreement (renewal)

renewal.terms: One additional term: shorter of 10 years or remaining lease term
conditions: Written notice of intent to renew
Sign then-current form of franchise agreement (may differ materially, including higher or additional fees)
In good standing with no history of substantial noncompliance
Have right to remain in possession of restaurant premises throughout renewal period
renewal.risk_flag: Then-current agreement may differ materially — significant re-pricing risk

fee_obligations.computations: Based on Gross Sales as defined in Section 16.12 (Section 9.4)
fee_obligations.weekly_payment: ADQ may require weekly payment if late (Section 9.5)
fee_obligations.eft: Must participate in electronic funds transfer (Section 9.6)
fee_obligations.interest_late_fees: 18% per annum or max by law; $50 per delinquent item (Section 9.7)

site_and_construction.building_plans: Must comply with ADQ design criteria, ADA, all laws. ADQ written approval before construction (Section 5.1)
site_and_construction.modernization: Required at renewal, on transfer (certain circumstances), and every 10 years or shorter lease period (Section 5.5)
site_and_construction.relocation: Only within 500 meters (emergency) or 2 miles (relocation policy, Street only) (Section 5.4)
site_and_construction.lease_approval: ADQ has right but not obligation to approve lease before execution (Section 5.6)

training_and_operations.pre_opening_training: 3 required attendees must complete all phases of training (Section 7.1)
training_and_operations.ongoing_training: ADQ may require additional training at franchisee expense (Section 7.2)
training_and_operations.training_on_default: ADQ may require training as cure condition (Section 7.3)
training_and_operations.meeting_attendance: Controlling owner must attend all ADQ meetings in area/region (Section 7.7)
training_and_operations.menu_control: ADQ has right to determine authorized menu; may require max/min/other pricing (Section 6.1)
training_and_operations.operations_manual: Confidential, 469 pages, ADQ can modify without consent (Section 6.11)
training_and_operations.epos_system: ADQ-designated, unlimited access to data, no limits on upgrade frequency/cost (Section 6.5)
training_and_operations.hours_of_operation: Section 6.9(A) — must be open to public during specified hours
training_and_operations.health_sanitation: Section 6.7 — must comply with all health and sanitation requirements

by_franchisor_cure_periods.24_hours: Default materially impairing trademark goodwill or threat to public health/safety (Section 13.2(B))
by_franchisor_cure_periods.10_days: Failure to provide required reports or pay amounts due (Section 13.2(A))

non_curable_defaults: Loss of right to occupy premises
Failure to reopen after restaurant destroyed/damaged
Conviction of offense related to restaurant
Named as specially designated national or blocked person by OFAC

termination_fee.formula_primary: Two times continuing license fee for last 12 months of active operations
termination_fee.formula_short_operation: If <12 months operation: 24 times average monthly continuing license fee
termination_fee.formula_near_expiration: If <24 months remaining: remaining months times average monthly fee

termination.by_franchisee: Section 13.3 — May terminate for material breach by ADQ; 30 days written notice; ADQ has 30 days to cure; effective 60 days after notice

transfer_restrictions.definition: Includes transfer of restaurant, assets, revenues, direct/indirect ownership, or management agreement (Section 11.2)
conditions: All owners of transferee sign guarantee (Section 11.3(H))
Transferee signs then-current agreement (may differ materially) (Section 11.3(L))
transfer_restrictions.right_of_first_refusal: Section 11.3(B) — ADQ can match any offer. If insolvency/bankruptcy, appraiser sets price.
transfer_restrictions.consent_standard: Not unreasonably withheld if all conditions met (Section 11.4)
transfer_restrictions.void_transfers: Any transfer without consent is void (Section 11.5)

cross_default.notes: Default under any franchise agreement or lease can trigger termination of MultiTRA (MultiTRA Section 9B). Within single franchise agreement, third default in 12 months is non-curable.

during_term.scope: No direct or indirect involvement in operation of any quick service restaurant that serves hamburgers but does not serve alcohol, or a business generating >10% of revenue from ice cream, yogurt, frozen custard, fruit beverages, soft-serve or other frozen treats (Competitive Business)

post_term.scope: No direct or indirect involvement in Competitive Business
post_term.geographic_street: Within 500 meters of authorized location for 1 year after termination/expiration
post_term.state_overrides: CA (may be unenforceable), ND (may be unenforceable), WA (void below $100K employee earnings)

guaranty.scope: All entity owners must sign personal undertaking and guarantee
guaranty.type: Joint and several, unlimited personal guarantee
guaranty.spousal: Not explicitly stated in Item 17 tables but Undertaking and Guarantee exhibit likely includes
guaranty.survives_transfer: Obligations survive until transferee completes all requirements

liquidated_damages.termination_fee: 2× last 12 months' royalty functions as liquidated damages. CA addendum notes may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671. ND addendum deletes entirely.

dispute_resolution.arbitration: Section 12.1 — All disputes except certain claims must be arbitrated in Minneapolis, MN or mutually agreeable place
dispute_resolution.injunctive_relief: Section 12.2 — ADQ may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction
dispute_resolution.attorneys_fees: Section 12.3 — Prevailing party entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees
dispute_resolution.venue: Section 15.9 — Federal District Court for District of MN or Hennepin County, Minneapolis
dispute_resolution.governing_law: Section 15.8 — Law of state where authorized location is situated
dispute_resolution.punitive_damages_waiver: Section 15.10 — Waiver of exemplary and punitive damages

state_overrides.CA: Arbitration venue may be challenged under CA B&P Code §20040.5
state_overrides.IL: Forum selection outside IL void (except arbitration)
state_overrides.MD: Claims under MD law may be brought in MD
state_overrides.MN: Franchise law prohibits requiring litigation outside MN, waiver of jury trial, consent to injunctive relief, liquidated damages
state_overrides.ND: Arbitration at nearest AAA office; punitive damages waiver unenforceable; jury waiver unenforceable
state_overrides.RI: Venue/jurisdiction outside RI void under RI Act
state_overrides.WA: Arbitration in WA or mutual agreement; litigation in WA not precluded

insurance.minimum: Liability insurance at minimum limit designated by ADQ periodically
insurance.additional: Any insurance required by law or related agreements
insurance.ADQ_arranged: Third party insurer makes insurance available to qualifying franchisees

indemnification.scope: Licensee indemnifies ADQ and affiliates. ADQ is not liable for franchisee's business operations.

force_majeure.noted: Force majeure provision exists but specific terms not detailed in Item 17 summary

distinctive_clauses.data_security: Section 6.16 — Must comply with PCI Data Security Standards
distinctive_clauses.payment_methods: Section 6.15 — Must accept all payment methods designated by ADQ
distinctive_clauses.improvements_belong_to_adq: Section 6.13 — Any improvements or materials developed by franchisee become ADQ property
distinctive_clauses.internet_restrictions: Section 3.4 — No independent website or social media without ADQ approval
distinctive_clauses.sales_reconstruction: Section 9.10(D) — ADQ may reconstruct sales if records are inadequate
distinctive_clauses.purchase_option: Section 14.5 — Upon termination/expiration, ADQ may purchase or designate third party to purchase restaurant assets at appraised value
distinctive_clauses.time_limit_on_claims: Section 14.8 — Time period for bringing claims (specific limitation not detailed in Item 17)
